commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add create task dialog, auto-save, multi-file drop, and comments/data fixes

- Add CreateTaskDialog: when filters are active, Enter opens a dialog instead
  of inserting an inline block, supporting #hashtag and @mention parsing
- Add debounced auto-save (3s default) for inline note title edits
- Support multi-file drag & drop in BlockNoteEditor with parallel uploads,
  and inject delete buttons on file blocks
- Simplify comments UI: remove reply/resolve threading, flatten to a linear
  comment list with compact layout and toast on save
- Make comments fully optimistic: instant UI updates with pending-mutation
  guard to prevent realtime subscription from overwriting local state
- Fix foreign key violations: await note insert in Supabase before emitting
  label/assignee events in createNote and createNoteAfter
- Add optimistic assignee cache updates via event subscription
- Change default note sort from sort_order to created_at descending
- Fix GCal calendar fetch deduplication with one-shot ref
- Add is_default field to Contact type, fix Supabase type casts
- Remove note_comments updated_at DB trigger (managed in app code)
- Add i18n keys for create task dialog and comment saved toast (en/es/pt)

Add terminal insert/delete line support for efficient line operations

Add insertLines(n) and deleteLines(n) static methods using CSI L and CSI M
escape sequences. These allow efficient handling of line insertion and
deletion by shifting existing terminal content rather than redrawing.

Works within scroll regions when set, enabling split-screen aware updates.

commit.message:
Add auto-scaling fonts + table-based sticker template

Template (v3):
- Table layout with 2 rows at exactly 5.15" each (fixed height)
- Content physically cannot overflow to page 2
- Vertically centered, no visible borders

Backend auto-scaling:
- New _auto_scale_paragraph() runs after placeholder replacement
- Estimates text width using char-count heuristic (avg_width = 0.55 * font_size)
- Shrinks font when replacement text exceeds available page width
- Minimum font size 14pt, scales gracefully for long facility names
- Reads actual page margins from the DOCX for accurate width calculation

Add hazard system with comets, solar flares, and gravity wells

Introduce three new hazard types for campaign progression:
- Comets: fast-moving projectiles with particle trails
- Solar flares: radial arc pulses from the star with warning phase
- Gravity wells: vortexes that pull nearby debris toward them

Hazards are gradually introduced across campaign zones:
Zone 2 (levels 5-8): comets only
Zone 3 (levels 5-8): comets + solar flares
Zone 4 (levels 3-8): all three hazards
Zone 5 (all levels): all hazards at high intensity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add standalone R-toggle 60fps patch with .text code cave

Replaces broken .data code cave with a proper .text code cave at 0x104810
(verified dead function, zero references in entire binary). The code cave
reads R button state from the game's input global at [*(0x3F71C0)+0x61C28]
and conditionally returns 30fps (R held) or 60fps (R released).

Works on real 3DS hardware - no emulator mods needed.

Verified: 63.5 avg FPS headless (PASS), no crash.
159 bytes, 11 IPS records, 52-byte code cave.

Fix Keystatic GitHub mode: use NODE_ENV instead of server-only env var

The storage mode check used KEYSTATIC_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID which is not
available in the client bundle (no NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix), causing the
Keystatic UI to fall back to local mode and request /api/keystatic/tree
instead of calling the GitHub API directly.

Resolve pre-development advisory items 1.1 and 1.2

1.1 - Meeting observation & live meeting manager UX: Documented
firsthand domain expertise (15 years as elected official), derived
10 design principles for the live meeting manager, and proposed
distributed audio capture feature with formal/informal modes.

1.2 - Roles & permissions model: Replaced spec's conflated roles
with 4 clean app roles (sys_admin, admin, staff, board_member),
researched Maine conflict-of-interest law (30-A MRSA §2605),
adopted PERSON entity architecture, built 30-action permissions
matrix with 5 default templates, and established board member
communication rules for Open Meetings Law compliance.

commit.message:
fix: disable Next.js fetch cache for Supabase admin client

Next.js 14 caches fetch() responses by default (Data Cache). The
Supabase JS client uses fetch internally, so PostgREST responses
were being silently cached — causing newly inserted bookings to
be invisible from Vercel serverless functions despite being present
in the database.

Pass cache: "no-store" via the global fetch override to ensure
every Supabase query hits the database fresh.

commit.message:
Fix PKCE: generate code_verifier in start-oauth and pass to token exchange

Root cause: google-auth-oauthlib adds code_challenge to auth URL but
handle_oauth_callback creates a NEW flow (loses the code_verifier),
causing Google to return 'invalid_grant: Missing code verifier'

Fix:
- start_google_drive_oauth now explicitly generates PKCE code_verifier
  and code_challenge, adds them to the authorization URL, and stores
  code_verifier in Flask session
- google_oauth_callback retrieves code_verifier from session and passes
  it to handle_oauth_callback
- handle_oauth_callback accepts optional code_verifier param and passes
  it to flow.fetch_token when available
- settings.py callback also retrieves and passes code_verifier

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Hide sprite bubble instantly on click to prevent carryover into next page

commit.message:
fix: split fanzone.sql into core + quiz to avoid 262KB ConfigMap limit

Split the monolithic fanzone.sql (345KB) into two files that each stay
under the 262KB annotation limit:
- fanzone.sql (178KB) — core schema, tables, seed data
- fanzone__quiz.sql (167KB) — quiz tables + seed questions

The bootstrap script now strips __* suffixes from filenames to derive
the target database name, so fanzone__quiz.sql applies to the fanzone
database. Removed ServerSideApply workaround since it's no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Migration: restructure stages 28 -> 8, remove subprocesses

Each of 4 main processes now has exactly 2 stages:
- Анализ: Анализ + Передача в исполнение [FINAL]
- Исполнение: В работе + Завершение исполнения [FINAL]
- Проверка: Проверка + Формирование вердикта [FINAL]
- Закрепление: Закрепление + Архивирование [FINAL]

Deleted: 20 intermediate stages, 27 subprocesses with 81 stages,
Архивирование process with 4 stages, 11 stage_transitions.
Work items on deleted stages moved to working stage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add not_applicable status and phase-aligned goals to scoring

Patterns irrelevant to an architecture (e.g., Agent Calls Human for
batch pipelines) can now be marked "not_applicable" — they don't block
maturity level progression or appear in gap analysis.

Score architecture now produces phase-aligned goals: each pattern gets
a phase field (1-based) tying it to its implementation roadmap phase,
and the Goal column covers the full roadmap scope via the new
roadmap_levels parameter (default 3) instead of just the immediate
target level.

commit.message:
Replace NativeBackend with OpenCode as agent execution foundation (#10)

* Add OpenCode agent SDK replacement design doc

Documents the decision to replace NativeBackend with OpenCode as the
execution foundation, covering architecture, event normalization,
session mapping, tool config, and migration path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add OpenCode backend implementation plan

11-task plan covering: deps swap, types rewrite, SessionIdMap,
ConfigGenerator, OpenCodeBackend, DashAgent simplification,
config schema update, agent-server wiring, and cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: swap @dash/llm for @opencode-ai/sdk

- Replace @dash/llm dependency in packages/agent/package.json with @opencode-ai/sdk ^1.2.20
- Remove packages/llm from root workspaces (expand glob to explicit list)
- Remove -w packages/llm from root build script

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove @dash/llm from app deps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(agent): rewrite types for OpenCode backend

Replace @dash/llm-dependent Session/SessionStore/Tool types with clean
OpenCode-oriented AgentEvent, AgentState, DashAgentConfig, and AgentBackend
interfaces. Expand usage type with cache token fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent): make cache token usage fields optional

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(agent): add SessionIdMap for OpenCode session tracking

Maps Dash channelId:conversationId keys to OpenCode session UUIDs,
rebuilt from the OpenCode session list on startup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(agent): add model parser and tool map builder

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent): fix SessionIdMap race condition and export interface

- Export SessionClient interface (was unexported)
- Add in-flight promise deduplication to prevent concurrent getOrCreate
  calls for the same key from creating duplicate sessions
- Clear stale map entries on re-init with this.map.clear()
- Improve idempotency test with call counter to verify single create call
- Add concurrent deduplication test using Promise.all

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(agent): add OpenCodeBackend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(agent): remove SessionStore from DashAgent

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(agent): update exports for OpenCode backend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(dash): update config schema for OpenCode

- AgentConfig: remove maxTokens and thinking fields; add provider/model comment
- DashJsonConfig: remove sessions field (OpenCode owns session storage)
- CredentialsConfig: replace per-provider apiKey fields with providerApiKeys map
- DashConfig: replace anthropicApiKey/googleApiKey/openaiApiKey/sessionDir with providerApiKeys map
- DEFAULTS: update model to anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5 format; update tools to OpenCode names
- loadConfig: replace per-provider credential resolution with credEnvMap loop; remove anthropicApiKey missing-key error; remove sessionDir from return value

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(dash): wire OpenCodeBackend in agent-server

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(dash): wire secrets file provider keys into providerApiKeys

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove NativeBackend, SessionStore, old tools, and packages/llm

OpenCode handles execution, tool invocation, and session storage natively.
These packages are replaced by OpenCodeBackend, SessionIdMap, and ConfigGenerator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix build, test, and lint errors after OpenCode migration

- Fix SSE stream access: await event.subscribe() and destructure
  {stream} before iterating (TS2504 error)
- Update TUI app to use OpenCodeBackend instead of deleted
  NativeBackend/AnthropicProvider/JsonlSessionStore
- Update config tests to use new providerApiKeys schema instead of
  anthropicApiKey, remove sessionDir assertions
- Fix biome formatting across all new files (array expansion, line
  width, parenthesized typeof, trailing commas)
- Fix biome lint: replace as any with biome-ignore comments in SDK
  interop code, type test mocks with SessionClient interface
- Remove unused thinking/maxTokens from TUI config interfaces
